config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama; kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, mudah sekali berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan, suara minoritas, dan martabat orang per orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar individu secara sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat, lalu kepentingan umum diatur di atas dasar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify overreach, censorship, and abuse of minorities. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require limits that affect everyone. The real standard should be whether a restriction is narrow, necessary, and fair, not whether “society” is simply declared more important by default. If we lose that balance, the phrase “common good” becomes a blank check to trample people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dijaga adalah hak dasar, terutama bagi kelompok kecil atau minoritas. Memang benar ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang butuh pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan adil, bukan memberi cek kosong kepada negara atau mayoritas. Buat saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dihormati dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun tanpa menginjak martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States “society first” has often been a convenient excuse for government overreach and for ignoring unpopular people. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are a license to do anything at all; public health, safety, and basic civil order can justify limited restrictions when they are narrow and genuinely necessary. What I reject is the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual by default, because that turns the phrase “common good” into a tool that can be abused by the majority. In my view, rights should be the starting point, and any limit on them should have a very strong justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga, apalagi minoritas, jadi saya curiga kalau kalimat itu dijadikan prinsip utama tanpa batas yang jelas. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, perlu, dan tidak boleh jadi alat mayoritas untuk bertindak semaunya. Bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi titik awal; kepentingan bersama baru sah kalau dibangun tanpa merampas martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of “society first” logic has too often been used to excuse overreach and silence unpopular people. I also do not think rights are unlimited in every situation, since public health, safety, and basic order can justify narrow restrictions when they are truly necessary. But the burden has to be on society to prove the limit is justified, not on the individual to prove why their rights should exist. In my view, rights should come first, and any exception should be tightly limited and carefully defended.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
